title: Task 1: Create Makefile with Core Commands
design:
## Goal

Create Makefile with core development commands: repl, dev, test, clean. This establishes the primary interface before adding linting/formatting.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Create Makefile

```makefile
.PHONY: repl dev test clean help

# Detect Java - try common locations
JAVA_HOME ?= $(shell \
  if [ -d "/usr/local/opt/openjdk" ]; then echo "/usr/local/opt/openjdk"; \
  elif [ -d "/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk" ]; then echo "/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk"; \
  elif [ -n "$$JAVA_HOME" ]; then echo "$$JAVA_HOME"; \
  else echo ""; fi)

ifneq ($(JAVA_HOME),)
  export PATH := $(JAVA_HOME)/bin:$(PATH)
endif

help:
	@echo "Fizzle Development Commands"
	@echo ""
	@echo "  make repl     - Start ClojureScript REPL (node)"
	@echo "  make dev      - Start browser dev server"
	@echo "  make test     - Run all tests"
	@echo "  make clean    - Remove build artifacts"
	@echo ""
	@echo "Validation (after linting added):"
	@echo "  make validate - Run all checks (lint + fmt + test)"

repl:
	npx shadow-cljs node-repl

dev:
	npx shadow-cljs watch app

test:
	npx shadow-cljs compile test && node out/test.js

clean:
	rm -rf out/ .shadow-cljs/ resources/public/js/
```

### 2. Update CLAUDE.md

Add this section after Build Commands:

```markdown
## Development Commands

Use these make commands instead of hand-rolling shadow-cljs calls:

\`\`\`bash
# Development
make repl      # Start node REPL - ready to eval immediately
make dev       # Start browser dev server with hot reload
make test      # Run all tests
make clean     # Remove build artifacts (out/, .shadow-cljs/)

# Validation (after Phase 2)
make validate  # Run lint + format-check + tests
\`\`\`

**IMPORTANT**: Always use \`make test\` instead of raw shadow-cljs commands.
```

### 3. Verify all commands work

- [ ] `make help` shows available commands
- [ ] `make repl` starts REPL, can eval `(+ 1 2)` and `(require '[fizzle.db.init :refer [init-game-state]])`
- [ ] `make test` runs 40 tests, all pass
- [ ] `make dev` starts watch (ctrl-c to exit)
- [ ] `make clean` removes out/ and .shadow-cljs/

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Makefile exists at project root with repl, dev, test, clean, help targets
- [ ] `make repl` starts REPL in one command without PATH export
- [ ] `make test` runs all 40 tests, outputs pass count
- [ ] `make help` displays all available commands
- [ ] `make clean` removes out/, .shadow-cljs/, resources/public/js/
- [ ] CLAUDE.md has "Development Commands" section with make commands
- [ ] CLAUDE.md instructs agent to use make commands
- [ ] Changes committed

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Java Not Installed**
- Makefile attempts to detect Java in common locations
- If Java not found, shadow-cljs will fail with clear error message
- User must install Java separately (documented in README)

**Edge Case: Different JAVA_HOME Locations**
- macOS Intel Homebrew: /usr/local/opt/openjdk
- macOS ARM Homebrew: /opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk
- Linux: Typically in PATH already or JAVA_HOME set
- Makefile checks multiple locations, falls back to existing JAVA_HOME/PATH

**Edge Case: npm not installed**
- npx requires Node.js/npm
- Error message will be clear: "npx: command not found"
- Prerequisite, not something Makefile can fix

**Portability**
- Makefile uses POSIX shell commands (works on macOS/Linux)
- Windows users need WSL or alternative (out of scope)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO hard-coded absolute paths without fallbacks (portability)
- ❌ NO manual PATH exports required before running make (usability)
- ❌ NO npm scripts duplicating Makefile commands (single source of truth)
- ❌ NO undocumented commands (help target lists all)

title: Task 1: Datascript Schema and Initial Game State
design:
## Goal

Create the Datascript schema and initial game state with Dark Ritual in hand, providing the foundation for all engine functions.

## Effort Estimate
4-6 hours

## Implementation

### 1. Project Setup (if needed)

Configure shadow-cljs.edn for test build:
```clojure
;; shadow-cljs.edn - add test build if not present
{:source-paths ["src/main" "src/test"]
 :dependencies [[datascript "1.6.5"]
                [reagent "1.2.0"]]
 :builds
 {:app {...}
  :test {:target :node-test
         :output-to "out/test.js"
         :ns-regexp ".*-test$"
         :autorun true}}}
```

### 2. Study existing patterns

Reference design doc schema (fizzle-design.md lines 231-274):
- Card definitions with :card/id, :card/name, :card/effects
- Game objects with :object/id, :object/zone, :object/card
- Players with :player/id, :player/mana-pool, :player/storm-count
- Game state with :game/phase, :game/active-player

### 3. Write tests first (TDD)

```clojure
;; test/fizzle/db/schema_test.cljs

(ns fizzle.db.schema-test
  (:require [cljs.test :refer-macros [deftest testing is]]
            [datascript.core :as d]
            [fizzle.db.schema :refer [schema]]
            [fizzle.db.init :refer [init-game-state]]
            [fizzle.db.queries :as q]))

;; Schema validity tests
(deftest schema-creates-valid-connection-test
  (testing "schema creates connection that accepts transactions"
    (let [conn (d/create-conn schema)]
      (is (some? conn))
      ;; Actually verify we can transact - not just that conn exists
      (d/transact! conn [{:player/id :test-player
                         :player/life 20}])
      (is (= 20 (-> @conn
                    (d/q '[:find ?life .
                           :where [?e :player/id :test-player]
                                  [?e :player/life ?life]])))))))

;; Initial state tests
(deftest initial-state-has-player-with-empty-mana-pool-test
  (testing "player mana pool starts with all zeros"
    (let [db (init-game-state)]
      (is (= {:white 0 :blue 0 :black 0 :red 0 :green 0 :colorless 0}
             (q/get-mana-pool db :player-1))))))

(deftest initial-state-has-dark-ritual-in-hand-test
  (testing "Dark Ritual is in player's hand"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)]
      (is (= 1 (count hand)))
      (is (= "Dark Ritual" (:card/name (first hand)))))))

(deftest initial-state-has-zero-storm-count-test
  (testing "storm count starts at 0"
    (let [db (init-game-state)]
      (is (= 0 (q/get-storm-count db :player-1))))))

;; Query edge case tests
(deftest get-mana-pool-nonexistent-player-test
  (testing "get-mana-pool returns nil for nonexistent player"
    (let [db (init-game-state)]
      (is (nil? (q/get-mana-pool db :nonexistent-player))))))

(deftest get-hand-empty-returns-empty-vector-test
  (testing "get-hand returns empty vector when no cards in hand"
    (let [conn (d/create-conn schema)]
      (d/transact! conn [{:player/id :empty-player
                         :player/life 20
                         :player/mana-pool {:white 0 :blue 0 :black 0 
                                            :red 0 :green 0 :colorless 0}
                         :player/storm-count 0}])
      (is (= [] (q/get-hand @conn :empty-player))))))

(deftest get-card-by-object-id-test
  (testing "get-card retrieves card definition from game object"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          obj-id (:object/id (first hand))]
      (is (= "Dark Ritual" (:card/name (q/get-card db obj-id)))))))
```

### 4. Implementation checklist

- [ ] shadow-cljs.edn - Add :test build and datascript dependency
  - Target: :node-test for fast CLI testing
  - Dependencies: datascript 1.6.5
  
- [ ] src/main/fizzle/db/schema.cljs - Datascript schema
  ```clojure
  (def schema
    {;; Card definitions (templates)
     :card/id         {:db/unique :db.unique/identity}
     :card/name       {:db/index true}
     :card/cmc        {}
     :card/mana-cost  {}  ; {:black 1} for B cost
     :card/colors     {:db/cardinality :db.cardinality/many}
     :card/types      {:db/cardinality :db.cardinality/many}
     :card/text       {}
     :card/effects    {}  ; EDN vector of effect maps
     :card/keywords   {:db/cardinality :db.cardinality/many}
     
     ;; Game objects (instances of cards in a game)
     :object/id       {:db/unique :db.unique/identity}
     :object/card     {:db/valueType :db.type/ref}
     :object/zone     {}  ; :hand, :stack, :graveyard, :battlefield, :library, :exile
     :object/owner    {:db/valueType :db.type/ref}
     :object/controller {:db/valueType :db.type/ref}
     :object/tapped   {}
     
     ;; Players
     :player/id       {:db/unique :db.unique/identity}
     :player/name     {}
     :player/life     {}
     :player/mana-pool {}  ; {:white 0 :blue 0 :black 0 :red 0 :green 0 :colorless 0}
     :player/storm-count {}
     
     ;; Game state (singleton)
     :game/id         {:db/unique :db.unique/identity}
     :game/turn       {}
     :game/phase      {}  ; :main1, :main2, etc.
     :game/active-player {:db/valueType :db.type/ref}})
  ```

- [ ] src/main/fizzle/cards/iggy_pop.cljs - Dark Ritual definition
  ```clojure
  (def dark-ritual
    {:card/id :dark-ritual
     :card/name "Dark Ritual"
     :card/cmc 1
     :card/mana-cost {:black 1}
     :card/colors #{:black}
     :card/types #{:instant}
     :card/text "Add {B}{B}{B}."
     :card/effects [{:effect/type :add-mana
                     :effect/mana {:black 3}}]})
  ```

- [ ] src/main/fizzle/db/queries.cljs - Query helpers
  ```clojure
  (defn get-mana-pool [db player-id]
    (d/q '[:find ?pool .
           :in $ ?pid
           :where [?e :player/id ?pid]
                  [?e :player/mana-pool ?pool]]
         db player-id))
  
  (defn get-hand [db player-id]
    (let [player-eid (d/q '[:find ?e .
                           :in $ ?pid
                           :where [?e :player/id ?pid]]
                         db player-id)]
      (when player-eid
        (->> (d/q '[:find [(pull ?obj [* {:object/card [*]}]) ...]
                   :in $ ?owner
                   :where [?obj :object/owner ?owner]
                          [?obj :object/zone :hand]]
                 db player-eid)
             (vec)))))
  
  (defn get-storm-count [db player-id]
    (d/q '[:find ?count .
           :in $ ?pid
           :where [?e :player/id ?pid]
                  [?e :player/storm-count ?count]]
         db player-id))
  
  (defn get-card [db object-id]
    (d/q '[:find (pull ?card [*]) .
           :in $ ?oid
           :where [?obj :object/id ?oid]
                  [?obj :object/card ?card]]
         db object-id))
  ```

- [ ] src/main/fizzle/db/init.cljs - Initial state creation
  ```clojure
  (defn init-game-state []
    (let [conn (d/create-conn schema)]
      ;; Transact card definition
      (d/transact! conn [dark-ritual])
      
      ;; Transact player
      (d/transact! conn [{:player/id :player-1
                         :player/name "Player"
                         :player/life 20
                         :player/mana-pool {:white 0 :blue 0 :black 0 
                                            :red 0 :green 0 :colorless 0}
                         :player/storm-count 0}])
      
      ;; Get entity IDs for refs
      (let [player-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :where [?e :player/id :player-1]] @conn)
            card-eid (d/q '[:find ?e . :where [?e :card/id :dark-ritual]] @conn)]
        
        ;; Transact game object (Dark Ritual in hand)
        (d/transact! conn [{:object/id (random-uuid)
                           :object/card card-eid
                           :object/zone :hand
                           :object/owner player-eid
                           :object/controller player-eid}])
        
        ;; Transact game state
        (d/transact! conn [{:game/id :game-1
                           :game/turn 1
                           :game/phase :main1
                           :game/active-player player-eid}]))
      
      ;; Return db value (immutable snapshot)
      @conn))
  ```

- [ ] test/fizzle/db/schema_test.cljs - Unit tests (see above)

### 5. Verification

```bash
# Run tests
npx shadow-cljs compile test && node out/test.js

# Or from REPL:
npx shadow-cljs cljs-repl app
(require '[fizzle.db.init :as init])
(def db (init/init-game-state))
(require '[fizzle.db.queries :as q])
(q/get-hand db :player-1)  ;; => [{:card/name "Dark Ritual" ...}]
(q/get-mana-pool db :player-1)  ;; => {:white 0 :blue 0 :black 0 ...}
(q/get-storm-count db :player-1)  ;; => 0
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `npx shadow-cljs compile test` completes without errors
- [ ] `node out/test.js` shows all 7 tests passing
- [ ] init-game-state returns Datascript db value (verified: `(d/db? db)` is true)
- [ ] `(q/get-hand db :player-1)` returns vector with one Dark Ritual object
- [ ] `(q/get-mana-pool db :player-1)` returns `{:white 0 :blue 0 :black 0 :red 0 :green 0 :colorless 0}`
- [ ] `(q/get-storm-count db :player-1)` returns `0`
- [ ] `(q/get-card db obj-id)` returns Dark Ritual card definition
- [ ] `(q/get-mana-pool db :nonexistent)` returns `nil` (edge case)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO hardcoded entity IDs (use queries to find entities by :db/unique attributes)
- ❌ NO mutable state outside Datascript conn (all state in one place)
- ❌ NO inline card definitions in init.cljs (cards live in cards/ namespace)
- ❌ NO ignoring nil returns from queries (handle missing entities gracefully)
- ❌ NO skipping tests for edge cases (nonexistent players, empty hands)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Nonexistent Player**
- Queries must handle player-id that doesn't exist
- Return nil or empty collection, don't throw
- Test: `get-mana-pool` with `:nonexistent-player` returns nil

**Edge Case: Empty Hand**
- `get-hand` should return empty vector `[]`, not nil
- Ensures consistent type for consumers
- Test: Player with no objects in hand returns `[]`

**Edge Case: Object Without Card Reference**
- Shouldn't happen in normal flow, but `get-card` should handle
- Return nil if object has no :object/card ref
- Defensive programming for corrupted state

**Schema Evolution**
- Datascript schema is additive - can add attributes later
- Don't worry about getting every attribute perfect now
- Focus on attributes needed for Dark Ritual flow

**Datascript Quirks**
- `d/q` returns nil for scalar queries (`. `) when no match
- `d/q` returns empty set `#{}` for collection queries when no match
- Always use appropriate return type hint in query

**Reference Implementation**
- Datascript docs: https://github.com/tonsky/datascript
- Design doc schema: fizzle-design.md lines 231-274

title: Task 2: Mana System (add, pay, can-pay?)
design:
## Goal

Implement the mana system: add mana to pool, pay costs, and check if costs can be paid. This is foundational for casting spells.

## Effort Estimate
3-4 hours

## Context
Completed Task 1: Datascript schema and initial state with Dark Ritual in hand.
Discovery: Datascript pull queries return nested data under attribute key.

## Implementation

### 1. Write tests first (TDD)

```clojure
;; test/fizzle/engine/mana_test.cljs

(ns fizzle.engine.mana-test
  (:require [cljs.test :refer-macros [deftest testing is]]
            [datascript.core :as d]
            [fizzle.db.schema :refer [schema]]
            [fizzle.db.init :refer [init-game-state empty-mana-pool]]
            [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
            [fizzle.engine.mana :as mana]))

;; === add-mana tests ===

(deftest add-mana-adds-black-mana-test
  (testing "add-mana adds black mana to empty pool"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          db' (mana/add-mana db :player-1 {:black 3})]
      (is (= {:white 0 :blue 0 :black 3 :red 0 :green 0 :colorless 0}
             (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1))))))

(deftest add-mana-accumulates-test
  (testing "add-mana accumulates with existing mana"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          db' (-> db
                  (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 2})
                  (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 1}))]
      (is (= 3 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))))))

(deftest add-mana-multiple-colors-test
  (testing "add-mana handles multiple colors at once"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          db' (mana/add-mana db :player-1 {:black 2 :blue 1})]
      (is (= 2 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1))))
      (is (= 1 (:blue (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))))))

(deftest add-mana-empty-map-test
  (testing "add-mana with empty map returns unchanged db"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          db' (mana/add-mana db :player-1 {})]
      (is (= (q/get-mana-pool db :player-1)
             (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1))))))

;; === can-pay? tests ===

(deftest can-pay-empty-pool-test
  (testing "can-pay? returns false with empty pool"
    (let [db (init-game-state)]
      (is (false? (mana/can-pay? db :player-1 {:black 1}))))))

(deftest can-pay-exact-mana-test
  (testing "can-pay? returns true with exact mana"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 1}))]
      (is (true? (mana/can-pay? db :player-1 {:black 1}))))))

(deftest can-pay-excess-mana-test
  (testing "can-pay? returns true with excess mana"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 3}))]
      (is (true? (mana/can-pay? db :player-1 {:black 1}))))))

(deftest can-pay-wrong-color-test
  (testing "can-pay? returns false with wrong color mana"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:blue 5}))]
      (is (false? (mana/can-pay? db :player-1 {:black 1}))))))

(deftest can-pay-empty-cost-test
  (testing "can-pay? returns true for empty cost (free spell)"
    (let [db (init-game-state)]
      (is (true? (mana/can-pay? db :player-1 {}))))))

(deftest can-pay-zero-amount-test
  (testing "can-pay? returns true for zero amount cost"
    (let [db (init-game-state)]
      (is (true? (mana/can-pay? db :player-1 {:black 0}))))))

;; === pay-mana tests ===

(deftest pay-mana-removes-mana-test
  (testing "pay-mana removes mana from pool"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 3}))
          db' (mana/pay-mana db :player-1 {:black 1})]
      (is (= 2 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))))))

(deftest pay-mana-exact-payment-test
  (testing "pay-mana handles exact payment (pool goes to zero)"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 1}))
          db' (mana/pay-mana db :player-1 {:black 1})]
      (is (= 0 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))))))

(deftest pay-mana-empty-cost-test
  (testing "pay-mana with empty cost returns unchanged pool"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 3}))
          db' (mana/pay-mana db :player-1 {})]
      (is (= 3 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))))))
```

### 2. Implementation checklist

- [ ] mkdir -p src/main/fizzle/engine
- [ ] src/main/fizzle/engine/mana.cljs - Mana operations
  - (add-mana db player-id mana-map) -> db with updated pool
  - (can-pay? db player-id cost-map) -> boolean
  - (pay-mana db player-id cost-map) -> db with reduced pool
- [ ] mkdir -p src/test/fizzle/engine  
- [ ] test/fizzle/engine/mana_test.cljs - Unit tests (13 tests)

### 3. Key implementation details

```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.mana
  "Mana pool operations for Fizzle.
   
   All functions are pure: (db, args) -> db
   Caller must check can-pay? before calling pay-mana."
  (:require [datascript.core :as d]
            [fizzle.db.queries :as q]))

(defn add-mana
  "Add mana to a player's pool. Pure function: (db, args) -> db
   
   mana-to-add is a map like {:black 3} or {:black 2 :blue 1}.
   Empty map {} is a no-op."
  [db player-id mana-to-add]
  (if (empty? mana-to-add)
    db
    (let [player-eid (q/get-player-eid db player-id)
          current-pool (q/get-mana-pool db player-id)
          new-pool (merge-with + current-pool mana-to-add)]
      (d/db-with db [[:db/add player-eid :player/mana-pool new-pool]]))))

(defn can-pay?
  "Check if player can pay a mana cost.
   
   Returns true if player has sufficient mana of each color.
   Empty cost {} or zero amounts {:black 0} always return true."
  [db player-id cost]
  (if (empty? cost)
    true
    (let [pool (q/get-mana-pool db player-id)]
      (every? (fn [[color amount]]
                (>= (get pool color 0) amount))
              cost))))

(defn pay-mana
  "Remove mana from player's pool to pay a cost.
   
   IMPORTANT: Caller MUST verify can-pay? first.
   Calling pay-mana without sufficient mana will result in negative pool values.
   Empty cost {} is a no-op."
  [db player-id cost]
  (if (empty? cost)
    db
    (let [player-eid (q/get-player-eid db player-id)
          current-pool (q/get-mana-pool db player-id)
          new-pool (merge-with - current-pool cost)]
      (d/db-with db [[:db/add player-eid :player/mana-pool new-pool]]))))
```

### 4. Verification

```bash
export PATH="/usr/local/opt/openjdk/bin:$PATH"
npx shadow-cljs compile test && node out/test.js
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] add-mana adds mana to player's pool correctly
- [ ] add-mana accumulates with existing mana
- [ ] add-mana handles multiple colors
- [ ] add-mana with empty map is no-op
- [ ] can-pay? returns false with empty pool
- [ ] can-pay? returns true with sufficient mana
- [ ] can-pay? returns false with wrong color
- [ ] can-pay? returns true for empty cost {}
- [ ] can-pay? returns true for zero amount {:black 0}
- [ ] pay-mana removes mana from pool
- [ ] pay-mana with empty cost is no-op
- [ ] All 13 mana tests pass
- [ ] Functions are pure (db in, db out)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO mutation outside d/db-with (purity: all changes via transactions)
- ❌ NO throwing on insufficient mana (caller should check can-pay? first)
- ❌ NO hardcoded player IDs (parameterized)
- ❌ NO handling generic mana yet (defer to later task - Dark Ritual has no generic cost)

## Key Considerations (SRE Review)

**Edge Case: Empty Cost**
- {} means free spell (like Lotus Petal activation cost)
- {:black 0} also means no black needed
- Both should return true for can-pay? and be no-ops for pay-mana

**Edge Case: Nonexistent Player**
- add-mana/pay-mana will throw if player doesn't exist (q/get-player-eid returns nil)
- This is acceptable - invalid player-id is a programming error
- Tests assume valid player-id (covered by schema tests)

**Edge Case: Negative Mana**
- pay-mana could produce negative values if called without can-pay? check
- Documented in docstring: caller MUST verify can-pay? first
- Not defensive - trusts caller (engine code, not user input)

**Not Implemented Yet (Out of Scope)**
- Generic mana costs (e.g., {2}{B} where 2 can be any color)
- Dark Ritual costs just {:black 1}, no generic needed yet
- Will add when implementing cards with generic costs

**Reference Implementation**
- Design doc mana section: fizzle-design.md lines 347-363

title: Task 4: Effect Interpreter (execute-effect add-mana)
design:
## Goal

Implement the effect interpreter that executes card effects. Starting with :add-mana effect type, which is needed for Dark Ritual's resolution.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Context
Completed Task 3: Zone transitions (move-to-zone).
Discovery: Datascript d/db-with works well for zone changes. q/get-object pulls full entity with card reference.

We need an effect interpreter that:
- Takes a db and an effect map
- Dispatches on :effect/type
- Returns new db with effect applied

## Implementation

### 1. Write tests first (TDD)

```clojure
;; test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs

(ns fizzle.engine.effects-test
  (:require [cljs.test :refer-macros [deftest testing is]]
            [fizzle.db.init :refer [init-game-state]]
            [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
            [fizzle.engine.effects :as fx]))

;; === execute-effect :add-mana tests ===

(deftest execute-effect-add-mana-test
  (testing "execute-effect with :add-mana adds mana to player's pool"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          effect {:effect/type :add-mana
                  :effect/mana {:black 3}}
          db' (fx/execute-effect db :player-1 effect)]
      (is (= 3 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))))))

(deftest execute-effect-add-mana-multiple-colors-test
  (testing "execute-effect with :add-mana handles multiple colors"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          effect {:effect/type :add-mana
                  :effect/mana {:black 2 :red 1}}
          db' (fx/execute-effect db :player-1 effect)]
      (is (= 2 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1))))
      (is (= 1 (:red (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))))))

(deftest execute-effect-add-mana-accumulates-test
  (testing "execute-effect with :add-mana accumulates with existing mana"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          effect {:effect/type :add-mana
                  :effect/mana {:black 3}}
          db' (-> db
                  (fx/execute-effect :player-1 effect)
                  (fx/execute-effect :player-1 effect))]
      (is (= 6 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))))))

(deftest execute-effect-unknown-type-test
  (testing "execute-effect with unknown type returns db unchanged"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          effect {:effect/type :unknown-effect}
          db' (fx/execute-effect db :player-1 effect)]
      (is (= (q/get-mana-pool db :player-1)
             (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1))))))

(deftest execute-effect-nil-type-test
  (testing "execute-effect with nil type (missing key) returns db unchanged"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          effect {}  ;; no :effect/type key
          db' (fx/execute-effect db :player-1 effect)]
      (is (= (q/get-mana-pool db :player-1)
             (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1))))))
```

### 2. Implementation checklist

- [ ] Create src/test/fizzle/engine/effects_test.cljs - Unit tests (5 tests)
- [ ] Run tests RED (namespace not found)
- [ ] Create src/main/fizzle/engine/effects.cljs - Effect interpreter
  - (execute-effect db player-id effect) -> db with effect applied
  - defmulti dispatching on :effect/type
  - defmethod for :add-mana
  - defmethod :default returns db unchanged (unknown effects are no-ops)
- [ ] Run tests GREEN (all 5 pass)
- [ ] Commit changes

### 3. Key implementation details

```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.effects
  "Effect interpreter for Fizzle.

   Dispatches on :effect/type to execute card effects.
   All functions are pure: (db, args) -> db"
  (:require [fizzle.engine.mana :as mana]))

(defmulti execute-effect
  "Execute an effect on the game state.

   Arguments:
     db - Datascript database value
     player-id - The player who controls this effect
     effect - Map with :effect/type and effect-specific keys

   Returns:
     New db with effect applied.

   Unknown effect types return db unchanged (no-op)."
  (fn [_db _player-id effect] (:effect/type effect)))

(defmethod execute-effect :default
  [db _player-id _effect]
  ;; Unknown effect types are no-ops
  db)

(defmethod execute-effect :add-mana
  [db player-id effect]
  (mana/add-mana db player-id (:effect/mana effect)))
```

### 4. Verification

```bash
export PATH="/usr/local/opt/openjdk/bin:$PATH"
npx shadow-cljs compile test && node out/test.js
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] execute-effect with :add-mana adds mana to player's pool
- [ ] execute-effect handles multiple colors in one effect
- [ ] execute-effect accumulates with existing mana
- [ ] execute-effect with unknown type returns db unchanged
- [ ] execute-effect with nil/missing type returns db unchanged
- [ ] All 5 effect interpreter tests pass
- [ ] Verification command passes: `npx shadow-cljs compile test && node out/test.js`

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Missing :effect/mana**
- If :add-mana effect has no :effect/mana key, passes nil to add-mana
- mana/add-mana uses (empty? mana-to-add) which returns true for nil
- Result: no-op, which is acceptable behavior

**Edge Case: Unknown Effect Type**
- Unknown types return db unchanged (silent no-op)
- This is per anti-patterns: engine trusts caller, rules layer validates
- defmulti :default method handles both unknown keywords and nil

**Edge Case: nil Effect Type (Missing Key)**
- If effect map has no :effect/type key, dispatch value is nil
- defmulti dispatches to :default method (no-op)
- Added explicit test to verify this behavior

**Architecture Note**
- Using defmulti for extensibility: adding new effect types = new defmethod
- Matches design doc's effect interpreter pattern
- player-id passed for effects that need to know who controls

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO throwing on unknown effect types (silent no-op per design)
- ❌ NO card-specific logic in interpreter (data-driven: card defines effect data)
- ❌ NO side effects (purity: interpreter transforms db)

title: Epic: Phase 1 Core Engine - Dark Ritual End-to-End
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)

- Dark Ritual can be cast from hand when player has B available
- Casting Dark Ritual moves it from hand → stack → graveyard
- Resolving Dark Ritual adds BBB to player's mana pool
- Mana pool tracks all five colors plus colorless
- Storm count increments when spells are cast
- Game state is queryable via Datascript
- All engine functions are pure: (db, args) -> db
- No UI required - all interaction via REPL

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)

- [ ] Can create initial game state with Dark Ritual in hand
- [ ] can-cast? returns true when player has B, false otherwise
- [ ] cast-spell moves card to stack and pays mana
- [ ] resolve-spell executes add-mana effect and moves to graveyard
- [ ] Mana pool correctly shows BBB after resolution
- [ ] Storm count is 1 after casting
- [ ] All functions testable via REPL with direct calls
- [ ] Unit tests pass for mana system
- [ ] Unit tests pass for zone transitions
- [ ] Unit tests pass for effect interpreter (add-mana)

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO internal randomness in engine functions (determinism: fork/replay requires all RNG as parameters)
- ❌ NO re-frame dependency in engine namespace (isolation: engine must be pure, re-frame wraps later)
- ❌ NO hardcoded card logic in engine (data-driven: cards are data, engine interprets)
- ❌ NO mutable state outside Datascript (simplicity: single source of truth)
- ❌ NO UI code in this phase (scope: REPL-only validates engine before UI work)
- ❌ NO skipping Datascript for 'simpler' atoms (architecture: Datascript enables declarative queries)

## Approach

Engine-first development with deferred re-frame integration. Build pure functions that transform Datascript state, validate via REPL and unit tests. The engine functions will later be wrapped by re-frame events, but remain independently testable.

All randomness passed as parameters to enable deterministic replay. Action granularity matches user intent (cast-spell is one action) even though engine does multiple db updates internally.

Start with Dark Ritual only - the simplest spell that exercises the full cast→resolve→effect pipeline.

## Architecture

```
src/fizzle/
├── db/
│   ├── schema.cljs     # Datascript schema for cards, objects, players, game
│   └── queries.cljs    # Common Datalog queries
├── engine/
│   ├── mana.cljs       # Mana pool: add, pay, can-pay?
│   ├── zones.cljs      # Zone transitions: move-to-zone
│   ├── rules.cljs      # Core rules: can-cast?, cast-spell, resolve-spell
│   └── effects.cljs    # Effect interpreter: execute-effect multimethod
└── cards/
    └── iggy_pop.cljs   # Card definitions (starting with Dark Ritual)
```

Key data flow:
1. Initial state created with Datascript schema + Dark Ritual in hand
2. can-cast? queries db for card in hand + sufficient mana
3. cast-spell: pay-mana → move-to-zone :stack → increment storm
4. resolve-spell: execute-effects → move-to-zone :graveyard
5. Effect interpreter dispatches on :effect/type, executes :add-mana

## Design Rationale

### Problem
Need a working game engine foundation before building UI, fork/replay, or additional cards. Dark Ritual is the simplest spell that validates the entire pipeline.

### Research Findings

**Codebase:**
- Project bootstrapped with shadow-cljs but no source files yet
- Design doc at fizzle-design.md defines schema, effect system, architecture
- src/main/ and src/test/ directories exist but are empty

**Design Doc Key Patterns:**
- Datascript schema (fizzle-design.md lines 231-274)
- Effect interpreter multimethod (lines 480-528)
- Card definition format (lines 277-345)
- Engine function signatures (lines 435-475)

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Engine-First with Deferred re-frame ✓

**What it is:** Build pure engine functions first, drive from REPL, add re-frame wrapper later.

**Investigation:**
- Reviewed design doc - engine functions are pure (db, args) -> db
- re-frame layer is thin wrapper around engine calls
- Testing pure functions simpler than testing through re-frame

**Pros:**
- Maximum testability
- Faster iteration on engine design
- Clear separation of pure logic vs app wiring

**Cons:**
- Delays validating re-frame integration

**Chosen because:** Testing is priority; re-frame wrapper is mechanical given clear engine API.

#### 2. Full re-frame from Start ❌

**What it is:** Wire events, subscriptions, and effects immediately alongside engine.

**Why we looked at this:** Validates integration early, events become action log.

**Investigation:**
- More ceremony for REPL-driven development
- Tests need re-frame test helpers OR test pure functions anyway
- Design doc already specifies event structure clearly

**Pros:**
- Validates event design early
- No integration surprises

**Cons:**
- More upfront wiring
- Tests more complex
- Slower iteration

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Given detailed design doc, integration risk is low. Pure engine functions are the hard work; re-frame wrapper is mechanical.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** We discover engine APIs that don't compose well with re-frame's event model.

### Scope Boundaries

**In scope:**
- Datascript schema for cards, objects, players, mana
- Mana system (pool, payment, generation)
- Zone transitions (hand → stack → graveyard)
- Effect interpreter (add-mana only)
- Dark Ritual card definition
- Unit tests for all engine functions

**Out of scope (deferred):**
- re-frame events/subscriptions - Phase 1b
- UI components - Phase 1c
- Additional cards (Cabal Ritual, Brain Freeze) - after Dark Ritual works
- Storm copies - after basic storm count works
- Fork/replay - Phase 3

### Open Questions

- Exact Datascript entity ID generation strategy (random UUID vs sequential?)
- Should card definitions live in Datascript or separate registry? (design doc shows both)

## Design Discovery (Reference Context)

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| MVP scope? | Single card (Dark Ritual) | Smallest vertical slice through full pipeline |
| re-frame/Datascript integration? | Datascript inside app-db | Single atom when re-frame added |
| UI priority? | REPL-only first | No UI code in Phase 1 |
| re-frame timing? | Defer to later | Engine functions pure, testable in isolation |

### Constraints for Future Integration

1. **All randomness as parameters** - no internal rand calls
2. **Functions are (db, explicit-args) -> db** - no hidden state
3. **Granularity matches user intent** - cast-spell is one conceptual action

### Dead-End Paths

None yet - this is greenfield implementation.

### Open Concerns Raised

- Integration risk when adding re-frame later → Mitigated by constraints above and clear design doc

title: Task 2: Add Linting and Formatting Commands
design:
## Goal

Add clj-kondo linting and cljstyle formatting to the Makefile. This completes the validation toolchain.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Context
Completed Task 1: Makefile exists with repl, dev, test, clean, help targets. Java detection working.

## Implementation

### 1. Install clj-kondo via npm

```bash
npm install --save-dev clj-kondo
```

Verify: `npx clj-kondo --version` shows version number.

### 2. Install cljstyle via Homebrew

cljstyle has NO npm package. Install via Homebrew cask:

```bash
brew install --cask cljstyle
```

**Post-install (macOS Gatekeeper):** Binary is unsigned, may need:
```bash
xattr -d com.apple.quarantine /usr/local/bin/cljstyle
```

Verify: `cljstyle version` shows version number.

### 3. Add Makefile targets

```makefile
.PHONY: lint fmt-check fmt validate

lint:
	npx clj-kondo --lint src/

fmt-check:
	cljstyle check src/

fmt:
	cljstyle fix src/

validate: lint fmt-check test
```

### 4. Run lint on existing code and fix issues

Run `make lint` on existing code. If errors found:
- Fix legitimate issues in source files
- Add exceptions to .clj-kondo/config.edn ONLY for false positives
- Do NOT suppress real errors

### 5. Run fmt-check on existing code and fix formatting

Run `make fmt-check` on existing code. If formatting issues found:
- Run `make fmt` to auto-fix
- Review changes before committing
- Commit formatting fixes separately from Makefile changes

### 6. Create .clj-kondo/config.edn (if needed for exceptions)

Only create if false positives need suppression:

```clojure
{:linters {:unused-namespace {:level :warning}
           :unused-binding {:level :warning}}}
```

### 7. Update help target

Add new commands to help output:

```makefile
help:
	@echo "Fizzle Development Commands"
	@echo ""
	@echo "  make repl      - Start ClojureScript REPL (node)"
	@echo "  make dev       - Start browser dev server"
	@echo "  make test      - Run all tests"
	@echo "  make lint      - Run clj-kondo linter"
	@echo "  make fmt-check - Check code formatting"
	@echo "  make fmt       - Auto-fix code formatting"
	@echo "  make validate  - Run lint + fmt-check + test"
	@echo "  make clean     - Remove build artifacts"
```

### 8. Verify all commands work

- [ ] `make lint` runs clj-kondo, exits 0 (no errors) or non-zero (errors found)
- [ ] `make fmt-check` exits 0 (formatted) or non-zero (needs formatting)
- [ ] `make fmt` modifies files and exits 0
- [ ] `make validate` runs all three in sequence, fails fast on first error
- [ ] `make help` shows all new commands

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `npm ls clj-kondo` shows clj-kondo in devDependencies
- [ ] `npx clj-kondo --version` outputs version
- [ ] `cljstyle version` outputs version (installed via Homebrew)
- [ ] `make lint` exits 0 on clean code (all lint issues fixed)
- [ ] `make fmt-check` exits 0 on formatted code (all formatting fixed)
- [ ] `make fmt` auto-fixes and exits 0
- [ ] `make validate` runs lint, fmt-check, test in sequence
- [ ] `make validate` fails immediately if lint fails (doesn't continue to fmt-check)
- [ ] `make help` lists all commands including lint, fmt-check, fmt, validate
- [ ] All 40 existing tests still pass
- [ ] Changes committed (Makefile changes + any code fixes in separate commits)

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Existing Code Has Lint Errors**
- EXPECT this to happen - new linter on existing code
- Fix errors in code, don't suppress legitimate warnings
- May need to fix unused requires, shadowed vars, etc.
- Commit lint fixes separately from Makefile changes

**Edge Case: Existing Code Has Formatting Issues**
- EXPECT this to happen - new formatter on existing code
- Run `make fmt` to auto-fix all formatting
- Review diff before committing
- Commit formatting fixes separately from Makefile changes

**Edge Case: cljstyle Not Installed**
- cljstyle is NOT available via npm
- Makefile will fail with clear error if cljstyle not found
- Document Homebrew installation in error message or README
- Fallback: Could check for cljstyle and skip if not found, but this defeats purpose

**Edge Case: macOS Gatekeeper Blocks cljstyle**
- Binary is unsigned, macOS may block execution
- Solution: `xattr -d com.apple.quarantine /usr/local/bin/cljstyle`
- Document in README or show in error message

**Portability**
- clj-kondo: npx works cross-platform (npm is prerequisite anyway)
- cljstyle: Homebrew is macOS-only; Linux users download binary manually
- Windows: Out of scope (WSL recommended)

**validate Target Behavior**
- MUST fail fast: if lint fails, don't run fmt-check or test
- Use `&&` chaining in Makefile: `validate: ; make lint && make fmt-check && make test`
- Alternative: Keep as dependencies but this runs all even on failure

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO suppressing real lint errors (fix the code, not the linter)
- ❌ NO auto-formatting on validate (fmt-check only; fmt is explicit action)
- ❌ NO hard-coded paths for tools (npx for clj-kondo, PATH for cljstyle)
- ❌ NO skipping lint/format steps even if tools missing (fail clearly)
- ❌ NO mixing Makefile changes with code fixes in same commit

title: Task 3: Zone Transitions (move-to-zone)
design:
## Goal

Implement zone transitions: moving game objects between zones (hand, stack, graveyard, battlefield, library, exile). This is foundational for cast-spell and resolve-spell.

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Context
Completed Task 2: Mana system (add, can-pay?, pay-mana).
Discovery: Datascript d/db-with returns new immutable db. Queries use pull for entity data with references.

## Implementation

### 1. Write tests first (TDD)

```clojure
;; test/fizzle/engine/zones_test.cljs

(ns fizzle.engine.zones-test
  (:require [cljs.test :refer-macros [deftest testing is]]
            [datascript.core :as d]
            [fizzle.db.init :refer [init-game-state]]
            [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
            [fizzle.engine.zones :as zones]))

;; === move-to-zone tests ===

(deftest move-to-zone-hand-to-stack-test
  (testing "move-to-zone moves object from hand to stack"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          object-id (:object/id (first hand))
          db' (zones/move-to-zone db object-id :stack)]
      (is (= :stack (:object/zone (q/get-object db' object-id))))
      (is (empty? (q/get-hand db' :player-1)))
      (is (= 1 (count (q/get-objects-in-zone db' :player-1 :stack)))))))

(deftest move-to-zone-stack-to-graveyard-test
  (testing "move-to-zone moves object from stack to graveyard"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          object-id (:object/id (first hand))
          db' (-> db
                  (zones/move-to-zone object-id :stack)
                  (zones/move-to-zone object-id :graveyard))]
      (is (= :graveyard (:object/zone (q/get-object db' object-id))))
      (is (empty? (q/get-objects-in-zone db' :player-1 :stack)))
      (is (= 1 (count (q/get-objects-in-zone db' :player-1 :graveyard)))))))

(deftest move-to-zone-preserves-other-attributes-test
  (testing "move-to-zone only changes zone, not owner/controller/card"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          object-id (:object/id (first hand))
          original-obj (q/get-object db object-id)
          db' (zones/move-to-zone db object-id :stack)
          moved-obj (q/get-object db' object-id)]
      (is (= (:object/owner original-obj) (:object/owner moved-obj)))
      (is (= (:object/controller original-obj) (:object/controller moved-obj)))
      (is (= (:object/card original-obj) (:object/card moved-obj))))))

(deftest move-to-zone-same-zone-no-op-test
  (testing "move-to-zone to same zone is a no-op"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          object-id (:object/id (first hand))
          db' (zones/move-to-zone db object-id :hand)]
      (is (= :hand (:object/zone (q/get-object db' object-id))))
      (is (= 1 (count (q/get-hand db' :player-1)))))))

(deftest move-to-zone-battlefield-test
  (testing "move-to-zone can move to battlefield"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          object-id (:object/id (first hand))
          db' (zones/move-to-zone db object-id :battlefield)]
      (is (= :battlefield (:object/zone (q/get-object db' object-id))))
      (is (= 1 (count (q/get-objects-in-zone db' :player-1 :battlefield)))))))

(deftest move-to-zone-exile-test
  (testing "move-to-zone can move to exile"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          object-id (:object/id (first hand))
          db' (zones/move-to-zone db object-id :exile)]
      (is (= :exile (:object/zone (q/get-object db' object-id))))
      (is (= 1 (count (q/get-objects-in-zone db' :player-1 :exile)))))))
```

### 2. Implementation checklist

- [ ] Create src/main/fizzle/engine/zones.cljs - Zone transition operations
  - (move-to-zone db object-id new-zone) -> db with object in new zone
- [ ] Create src/test/fizzle/engine/zones_test.cljs - Unit tests (6 tests)
- [ ] Run tests RED (namespace not found)
- [ ] Implement move-to-zone function
- [ ] Run tests GREEN (all 6 pass)
- [ ] Commit changes

### 3. Key implementation details

```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.zones
  "Zone transition operations for Fizzle.

   All functions are pure: (db, args) -> db"
  (:require [datascript.core :as d]
            [fizzle.db.queries :as q]))

(defn move-to-zone
  "Move a game object to a new zone. Pure function: (db, args) -> db

   Valid zones: :hand, :library, :graveyard, :stack, :battlefield, :exile
   Moving to the same zone is a no-op.
   
   IMPORTANT: Caller must ensure object-id exists. If object doesn't exist,
   query returns nil and d/db-with will fail."
  [db object-id new-zone]
  (let [current-zone (:object/zone (q/get-object db object-id))]
    (if (= current-zone new-zone)
      db
      (let [obj-eid (d/q '[:find ?e .
                           :in $ ?oid
                           :where [?e :object/id ?oid]]
                         db object-id)]
        (d/db-with db [[:db/add obj-eid :object/zone new-zone]])))))
```

### 4. Verification

```bash
export PATH="/usr/local/opt/openjdk/bin:$PATH"
npx shadow-cljs compile test && node out/test.js
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] move-to-zone moves object from hand to stack
- [ ] move-to-zone moves object from stack to graveyard
- [ ] move-to-zone preserves owner/controller/card references
- [ ] move-to-zone to same zone is no-op
- [ ] move-to-zone works for all zones (battlefield, exile)
- [ ] All 6 zone transition tests pass
- [ ] Verification command passes: `npx shadow-cljs compile test && node out/test.js`

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Nonexistent Object**
- If object-id doesn't exist, q/get-object returns nil
- Accessing `:object/zone` on nil returns nil
- Query for entity ID returns nil, d/db-with fails
- This is acceptable: invalid object-id is a programming error
- Caller (rules.cljs) must ensure object exists before calling

**Edge Case: Invalid Zone Keyword**
- No validation of zone keyword (could pass :invalid-zone)
- Datascript will accept any keyword value
- Queries for that zone will return empty results
- Rules layer should only pass valid zones; engine trusts caller

**Performance Note**
- q/get-object does a full pull with card reference
- For zone change, we only need current zone
- Could optimize with direct query if needed later
- Not a concern for MVP (single-card tests)

**Reference: Zone List**
Valid zones per design doc: :hand, :library, :graveyard, :stack, :battlefield, :exile

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO zone-specific side effects (purity: zone change is just a db update)
- ❌ NO validation of legal zone transitions (simplicity: engine trusts caller; rules layer validates)
- ❌ NO automatic triggers (scope: triggers are later; zone change is mechanical)
- ❌ NO defensive nil checks (trust caller: invalid object-id is programming error)

title: Epic: Best-in-Class Developer Experience
design:
## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)

- Single command REPL: `make repl` starts node REPL ready to eval ClojureScript
- Single command validation: `make validate` runs lint + format-check + tests, fails fast
- All dev commands documented in CLAUDE.md for agent use
- Makefile is primary interface (familiar to Clojure devs)
- clj-kondo for static analysis/linting
- cljstyle for code formatting (check and fix modes)

## Success Criteria (MUST ALL BE TRUE)

- [ ] `make repl` starts REPL in one command, ready to eval immediately
- [ ] `make test` runs all tests and reports pass/fail
- [ ] `make lint` runs clj-kondo on src/, reports issues
- [ ] `make fmt-check` verifies formatting without changes
- [ ] `make fmt` auto-fixes formatting
- [ ] `make validate` runs lint + fmt-check + test sequentially, fails on first error
- [ ] `make dev` starts browser watch mode
- [ ] `make clean` removes build artifacts
- [ ] CLAUDE.md updated with Development Commands section
- [ ] All existing tests still pass

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO multi-step REPL setup (simplicity: one command must work)
- ❌ NO npm scripts as primary interface (consistency: Makefile is standard for Clojure projects)
- ❌ NO manual PATH exports in commands (usability: Makefile handles Java path internally)
- ❌ NO hard-coded absolute paths (portability: must work on any machine)

## Approach

Create a Makefile that wraps shadow-cljs commands with proper Java PATH handling. Add clj-kondo via npm for linting and cljstyle as a standalone tool for formatting. Update CLAUDE.md with all commands so the agent uses them consistently.

## Architecture

```
Makefile              # Primary interface
├── repl              # npx shadow-cljs node-repl
├── dev               # npx shadow-cljs watch app  
├── test              # npx shadow-cljs compile test && node out/test.js
├── lint              # clj-kondo --lint src/
├── fmt-check         # cljstyle check src/
├── fmt               # cljstyle fix src/
├── validate          # lint && fmt-check && test
└── clean             # rm -rf out/ .shadow-cljs/

CLAUDE.md             # Agent instructions
└── Development Commands section
```

## Design Rationale

### Problem
Current DX requires multiple steps for REPL (watch + connect), complex test commands with PATH exports, and no linting/formatting. This slows development and causes friction.

### Research Findings

**External:**
- shadow-cljs node-repl provides one-command REPL for non-browser dev
- clj-kondo is the standard ClojureScript linter (2M+ weekly downloads)
- cljstyle is the recommended formatter (successor to cljfmt)
- Makefile is idiomatic for Clojure projects

### Approaches Considered

#### 1. Makefile with node-repl ✓

**What it is:** Use Makefile as primary interface, shadow-cljs node-repl for instant REPL.

**Chosen because:** Familiar to Clojure devs, one-command REPL, handles PATH internally.

#### 2. npm scripts ❌

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** Not familiar to Clojure devs, user explicitly requested Makefile.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** User changes preference.

#### 3. deps.edn aliases ❌

**⚠️ REJECTED BECAUSE:** shadow-cljs standalone is recommended over JVM invocation per official docs.

**🚫 DO NOT REVISIT UNLESS:** Need tight integration with other Clojure tooling.

### Scope Boundaries

**In scope:**
- Makefile with all dev commands
- clj-kondo linting
- cljstyle formatting
- CLAUDE.md updates

**Out of scope:**
- Editor integration (Calva/CIDER) - user can configure separately
- CI/CD setup - future epic
- Pre-commit hooks - future epic

## Design Discovery

### Key Decisions Made

| Question | User Answer | Implication |
|----------|-------------|-------------|
| CLI interface? | Makefile (familiar to Clojure devs) | Primary interface is make commands |
| REPL experience? | One command, auto-connects | Use node-repl, no browser needed |
| Validation scope? | All: format + lint + test | make validate runs everything |

title: Task 5: Core Rules (can-cast?, cast-spell, resolve-spell)
design:
## Goal

Implement the core rules functions that tie together mana, zones, and effects to enable the full Dark Ritual cast→resolve pipeline.

## Effort Estimate
3-4 hours

## Context
Completed Tasks 1-4: Schema, mana system, zone transitions, effect interpreter.

We have all the building blocks:
- mana/can-pay?, mana/pay-mana, mana/add-mana
- zones/move-to-zone
- effects/execute-effect with :add-mana

Now we need rules.cljs to orchestrate them into can-cast?, cast-spell, resolve-spell.

## Implementation

### 1. Write tests first (TDD)

```clojure
;; test/fizzle/engine/rules_test.cljs

(ns fizzle.engine.rules-test
  (:require [cljs.test :refer-macros [deftest testing is]]
            [fizzle.db.init :refer [init-game-state]]
            [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
            [fizzle.engine.mana :as mana]
            [fizzle.engine.zones :as zones]
            [fizzle.engine.rules :as rules]))

;; === can-cast? tests ===

(deftest can-cast-returns-false-without-mana-test
  (testing "can-cast? returns false when player lacks mana"
    (let [db (init-game-state)
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          ritual (first hand)]
      (is (false? (rules/can-cast? db :player-1 (:object/id ritual)))))))

(deftest can-cast-returns-true-with-mana-test
  (testing "can-cast? returns true when player has sufficient mana"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 1}))
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          ritual (first hand)]
      (is (true? (rules/can-cast? db :player-1 (:object/id ritual)))))))

(deftest can-cast-returns-false-wrong-zone-test
  (testing "can-cast? returns false when card not in hand (on stack)"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 2}))  ;; Extra mana for test
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          ritual (first hand)
          obj-id (:object/id ritual)
          ;; Move card to stack (simulating cast without using cast-spell)
          db' (zones/move-to-zone db obj-id :stack)]
      ;; Card is on stack, not in hand - should return false
      (is (false? (rules/can-cast? db' :player-1 obj-id))))))

;; === cast-spell tests ===

(deftest cast-spell-moves-to-stack-test
  (testing "cast-spell moves card from hand to stack"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 1}))
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          ritual (first hand)
          db' (rules/cast-spell db :player-1 (:object/id ritual))]
      (is (= 0 (count (q/get-hand db' :player-1))))
      (is (= 1 (count (q/get-objects-in-zone db' :player-1 :stack)))))))

(deftest cast-spell-pays-mana-test
  (testing "cast-spell deducts mana cost from pool"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 2}))
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          ritual (first hand)
          db' (rules/cast-spell db :player-1 (:object/id ritual))]
      (is (= 1 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))))))

(deftest cast-spell-increments-storm-test
  (testing "cast-spell increments storm count"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 1}))
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          ritual (first hand)
          db' (rules/cast-spell db :player-1 (:object/id ritual))]
      (is (= 1 (q/get-storm-count db' :player-1))))))

;; === resolve-spell tests ===

(deftest resolve-spell-executes-effects-test
  (testing "resolve-spell executes card effects"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 1}))
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          ritual (first hand)
          db' (-> db
                  (rules/cast-spell :player-1 (:object/id ritual))
                  (rules/resolve-spell :player-1 (:object/id ritual)))]
      ;; Dark Ritual adds BBB
      (is (= 3 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db' :player-1)))))))

(deftest resolve-spell-moves-to-graveyard-test
  (testing "resolve-spell moves card from stack to graveyard"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 1}))
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          ritual (first hand)
          db' (-> db
                  (rules/cast-spell :player-1 (:object/id ritual))
                  (rules/resolve-spell :player-1 (:object/id ritual)))]
      (is (= 0 (count (q/get-objects-in-zone db' :player-1 :stack))))
      (is (= 1 (count (q/get-objects-in-zone db' :player-1 :graveyard)))))))

;; === Full pipeline test ===

(deftest dark-ritual-full-pipeline-test
  (testing "Dark Ritual: cast with B, resolve to get BBB, storm = 1"
    (let [db (-> (init-game-state)
                 (mana/add-mana :player-1 {:black 1}))
          hand (q/get-hand db :player-1)
          ritual (first hand)
          obj-id (:object/id ritual)

          ;; Verify can cast
          _ (is (true? (rules/can-cast? db :player-1 obj-id)))

          ;; Cast it
          db-cast (rules/cast-spell db :player-1 obj-id)
          _ (is (= 0 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db-cast :player-1))))
          _ (is (= 1 (q/get-storm-count db-cast :player-1)))
          _ (is (= 1 (count (q/get-objects-in-zone db-cast :player-1 :stack))))

          ;; Resolve it
          db-resolved (rules/resolve-spell db-cast :player-1 obj-id)]
      (is (= 3 (:black (q/get-mana-pool db-resolved :player-1))))
      (is (= 0 (count (q/get-objects-in-zone db-resolved :player-1 :stack))))
      (is (= 1 (count (q/get-objects-in-zone db-resolved :player-1 :graveyard)))))))
```

### 2. Implementation checklist

- [ ] Create src/test/fizzle/engine/rules_test.cljs - Unit tests (9 tests)
- [ ] Run tests RED (namespace not found)
- [ ] Create src/main/fizzle/engine/rules.cljs - Core rules
  - (can-cast? db player-id object-id) -> boolean
  - (cast-spell db player-id object-id) -> db
  - (resolve-spell db player-id object-id) -> db
- [ ] Run tests GREEN (all pass)
- [ ] Commit changes

### 3. Key implementation details

```clojure
(ns fizzle.engine.rules
  "Core game rules for Fizzle.

   Orchestrates mana, zones, and effects into casting operations.
   All functions are pure: (db, args) -> db"
  (:require [datascript.core :as d]
            [fizzle.db.queries :as q]
            [fizzle.engine.mana :as mana]
            [fizzle.engine.zones :as zones]
            [fizzle.engine.effects :as effects]))

(defn can-cast?
  "Check if a player can cast a card.
   
   Requires:
   - Card is in player's hand
   - Player has sufficient mana
   
   Returns false if object doesn't exist."
  [db player-id object-id]
  (let [obj (q/get-object db object-id)]
    (when obj
      (let [card (:object/card obj)
            cost (:card/mana-cost card)]
        (and (= :hand (:object/zone obj))
             (mana/can-pay? db player-id cost))))))

(defn- increment-storm
  "Increment a player's storm count."
  [db player-id]
  (let [player-eid (q/get-player-eid db player-id)
        current (q/get-storm-count db player-id)]
    (d/db-with db [[:db/add player-eid :player/storm-count (inc current)]])))

(defn cast-spell
  "Cast a spell from hand.
   
   - Pays mana cost
   - Moves card to stack
   - Increments storm count
   
   Caller should verify can-cast? first."
  [db player-id object-id]
  (let [obj (q/get-object db object-id)
        card (:object/card obj)
        cost (:card/mana-cost card)]
    (-> db
        (mana/pay-mana player-id cost)
        (zones/move-to-zone object-id :stack)
        (increment-storm player-id))))

(defn resolve-spell
  "Resolve a spell on the stack.
   
   - Executes all effects
   - Moves card to graveyard"
  [db player-id object-id]
  (let [obj (q/get-object db object-id)
        card (:object/card obj)
        effects-list (:card/effects card)]
    (as-> db db'
      (reduce (fn [d effect] (effects/execute-effect d player-id effect))
              db'
              (or effects-list []))  ;; Handle nil effects gracefully
      (zones/move-to-zone db' object-id :graveyard))))
```

### 4. Verification

```bash
export PATH="/usr/local/opt/openjdk/bin:$PATH"
npx shadow-cljs compile test && node out/test.js
```

## Success Criteria

- [ ] can-cast? returns false when player lacks mana
- [ ] can-cast? returns true when player has sufficient mana
- [ ] can-cast? returns false when card not in hand (on stack)
- [ ] cast-spell moves card from hand to stack
- [ ] cast-spell deducts mana cost from pool
- [ ] cast-spell increments storm count
- [ ] resolve-spell executes card effects (adds BBB)
- [ ] resolve-spell moves card from stack to graveyard
- [ ] Full pipeline test passes: cast with B, resolve to get BBB, storm = 1
- [ ] All 9 rules tests pass
- [ ] Verification command passes: npx shadow-cljs compile test && node out/test.js

## Key Considerations (SRE REVIEW)

**Edge Case: Non-existent object-id**
- get-object returns nil for invalid object-id
- can-cast? wrapped in (when obj ...) to return nil/false gracefully
- cast-spell/resolve-spell: Caller should verify can-cast? first per contract
- Design decision: No defensive checks in cast-spell/resolve-spell (per anti-pattern "caller checks can-cast?")

**Edge Case: Card with nil effects**
- Dark Ritual has effects, but future cards might not
- resolve-spell uses (or effects-list []) to handle nil gracefully
- reduce on empty vector is no-op, safe

**Edge Case: Card with nil mana cost (free spell)**
- mana/can-pay? returns true for nil/empty cost (already tested in mana_test)
- mana/pay-mana handles nil/empty cost as no-op (already tested)
- No special handling needed in rules.cljs

**Why wrong-zone test requires zones/move-to-zone**
- Initial state has card in hand only
- Must move card to stack to test can-cast? returns false for non-hand cards
- Test explicitly calls zones/move-to-zone (not cast-spell) to isolate the can-cast? behavior
- Catches bug: can-cast? not checking :object/zone

**Function Contract: Caller Responsibility**
- can-cast? is safe to call with any inputs (returns false/nil gracefully)
- cast-spell and resolve-spell assume can-cast? was verified
- This matches MTG rules: you can't cast if not legal, validation happens before cast

## Anti-Patterns (FORBIDDEN)

- ❌ NO validation/throwing in cast-spell (caller checks can-cast?)
- ❌ NO card-specific logic in rules (effects are data-driven)
- ❌ NO side effects (purity: rules transform db)
- ❌ NO defensive nil checks in cast-spell/resolve-spell (violates contract)
